summary: Updated the repository's configured decomp-toolkit version from dtk v0.6.2 to v0.7.2 in configure.py. The PR was a small tooling/version bump with no discussion or review comments in the provided slice.

key_files.reason: Holds pinned tool versions; this PR changed config.dtk_tag from v0.6.2 to v0.7.2.

what_changed: In configure.py, config.dtk_tag was changed from "v0.6.2" to "v0.7.2".
No source files, headers, symbols, or matching code were changed in the provided diff.
smart_moves: Kept the tooling update isolated to the central tool-version configuration in configure.py.
Preserved the existing pattern of pinning external tool versions through config.*_tag fields alongside compilers_tag, sjiswrap_tag, and wibo_tag.
decomp_lessons: Centralized tool-version pins make infrastructure upgrades small, auditable, and easy to search.
For decompilation projects, dtk version changes should be tracked as meaningful build/matching infrastructure changes even when the diff is only a one-line configuration update.
naming_conventions: Tool version pins in configure.py use config.<tool>_tag naming, as seen with config.dtk_tag, config.compilers_tag, config.sjiswrap_tag, and config.wibo_tag.
dtk versions are recorded with a leading v tag string, e.g. "v0.7.2".
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or function-matching tactics were present in the PR slice.
The likely matching relevance is indirect: updating dtk may affect disassembly/decompilation tooling behavior, but the provided evidence does not describe any specific matching fix.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that changed config.dtk_tag in configure.py
Search for dtk v0.7.2 effects on matching or generated assembly
Compare build or decompilation output before and after dtk v0.7.2
